article_title: TypeScript 6.0 Ships as Final JavaScript-Based Release, Clearing the Path for Go-Native 7.0

content_preview.title: TypeScript 6.0 Ships as Final JavaScript-Based Release, Clearing the Path for Go-Native 7.0
content_preview.summary: Microsoft released TypeScript 6.0 on March 23, the last version of the compiler written in JavaScript. The release introduces strict-by-default settings and deprecates legacy features ahead of TypeScript 7.0, a full Go rewrite that has shown up to 10x faster compilation in benchmarks.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Microsoft released TypeScript 6.0 on March 23, 2026, closing a chapter that began with the language's debut in 2012. This is the final version of the TypeScript compiler written in JavaScript. No 6.1 release is planned; only patch-level fixes for critical security issues or severe regressions will follow. The entire TypeScript team is now focused on TypeScript 7.0, a ground-up rewrite of the compiler in Go that has already demonstrated compilation speedups of 8x to 13x across major ...

editor_notes.content_quality: Excellent technical article covering both TS 6.0 changes and TS 7.0 Go rewrite with detailed benchmarks and migration guidance.
editor_notes.source_verification: Microsoft DevBlog (native port): verified — Hejlsberg, Project Corsa, Go choice, VS Code speedups all confirmed. InfoQ: verified — strict defaults, nine changes, deprecations, ES2025 features confirmed. Microsoft DevBlog (Dec 2025 progress): verified — 10.4x VS Code, 10.1x Playwright, editor startup, 99.6% compatibility, VS Code extension confirmed.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All performance numbers, default changes, and deprecation details verified against official Microsoft sources.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral, technically precise, and actionable for developers.
editor_notes.originality: Fresh topic — no prior TypeScript 6.0 coverage.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Outstanding developer tools article with comprehensive technical detail from official sources. Approved.
